masthead.title: Give AI proven facts.
masthead.tagline: Every attribute your AI reads carries permanent provenance — who issued it, which schema defined it, how it was proven, and where the proof lives on-chain.

features.sectionHeading: Build on verified attributes, end to end.
features.encryptBody: Lemma keeps every document AES-GCM encrypted — your AI never touches raw PII. Only docHash and CID are exposed as stable anchors for provenance.
features.proveBody: Turn business rules like 'over 18' or 'revenue above threshold' into machine-checkable facts. Each verified proof is permanently recorded with its circuit and generator, so your AI always knows how a fact was proven.
features.discloseBody: Holders selectively reveal just the attributes your model requires for retrieval or ranking. The link to the original issuer signature stays intact, so provenance holds even when the view is partial.
features.queryBody: Ask 'users over 18 in Japan' and get back attributes with full provenance — proof status, schema, issuer, generator, and verification method — ready for your RAG policy layer.
features.schemaHeading: Define Your Domain as a Schema
features.schemaBody: Model how your AI retrieves and clusters knowledge — bucket ages, risk scores, regions — with typed schemas and normalization. Register ZK circuits and reproducible generators so every fact traces back to its source.
features.provenanceBody: Document commitments, schemas, issuers, and ZK verification results are anchored on-chain. Your RAG index can be rebuilt, your embeddings can be re-computed — the provenance layer stays permanent.

rebuild.title: Rebuild site
rebuild.intro: This page is not linked from the site. Use it to trigger a rebuild and redeploy of the static site (e.g. after updating content in Affine).
rebuild.option1Desc: Open the workflow page and click Run workflow.
rebuild.option2Desc: From a script or external service (e.g. after Affine updates), send a POST request with a Personal Access Token (PAT). This site is static, so a plain GET to this page cannot start the workflow without credentials.
rebuild.option2CurlNotePrefix: Store the PAT in your environment or a secret store; do not commit it. The workflow listens for

services.title: Lemma Oracle — Data Trust Infrastructure for Agent AI

hero.title: The real reason agent AI can't be used isn't the model—it's the data.
hero.sub: Generative AI adoption is accelerating. But results on the ground aren't keeping up. The root cause is the absence of a trusted data foundation.
hero.stat: About 90% of large domestic companies feel challenged by AI agent adoption

stats.stat1Desc: AI agent adoption challenges in large domestic companies (Japan survey)
stats.stat2Desc: Organizations struggling to use internal data with AI (Global survey)
stats.stat3Desc: Agent AI market size forecast for 2030 (40%+ annual growth)

problem.label: Overview of Challenges
problem.title: Why does AI stall on the front lines?
problem.lead: Generative AI is deployed but doesn't deliver results. The top challenge is concentrated on 'data.'
problem.issue1Content: Concerns about handling confidential and personal information
problem.issue2Content: Complexity of integrating with existing systems
problem.issue3Content: Not getting expected responses (data quality issues)
problem.issue4Content: Unclear output basis and inference process
problem.highlight: These appear as separate challenges, but the root converges on one point: 'Data that AI can trust and use is not prepared.'

walls.label: Structural Barriers
walls.title: Three Data Problems Agent AI Faces
walls.wall1Body: Sensor values, business logs, and contract records are exposed to loss and tampering risks as they pass through multiple points. Feeding them directly to AI induces hallucinations and distorts business reasoning.
walls.wall2Body: Handing over all data necessary for business automation to external parties is not permitted under personal information protection laws and confidentiality management. The contradiction of 'wanting to prove but not show contents' blocks AI utilization.
walls.wall3Body: If agent AI executes autonomously, humans must be able to verify and explain 'why that decision was made.' Traceability of processing grounds becomes a prerequisite for AI adoption.

solution.label: Solution
solution.title: What is Lemma Oracle
solution.lead: A 'data refinery infrastructure' that collects, verifies, and delivers real-world data to AI in a trusted form. Three functions—Normalize, Commit, Prove—provide a foundation where AI can safely execute business operations.
solution.func1Desc: Extract only attributes from encrypted documents via ZKP. AI agents can perform conditional reasoning, search, contracts, and payments without touching raw data.
solution.func2Desc: Identify issuers via DID and permanently record provenance information on-chain. Maintain a state where both AI and humans can audit and re-verify at any time.
solution.func3Desc: Prove only the 'fact that conditions are met' via ZKP without disclosing any confidential information. Can be safely presented to trading partners, audit bodies, and government agencies.
solution.badge: Compliant with the 'Trusted Web' design philosophy promoted by Japan's Digital Agency and Cabinet Secretariat

beforeAfter.label: Implementation Results
beforeAfter.title: What changes before and after implementation
beforeAfter.lead: Six critical operations — transformed from manual to cryptographically verified.
beforeAfter.before1Text: Manual visual inspection and manual matching
beforeAfter.before4Text: Stalled due to data quality concerns
beforeAfter.before5Text: Either disclose confidential info or give up proving
beforeAfter.after2Text: Automatically record condition achievement, human final confirmation
beforeAfter.after4Text: Safely deploy AI on verified foundation
beforeAfter.after5Text: Prove only 'facts' via ZKP, keep confidentiality
beforeAfter.after6Text: VC auto-issuance and status proof (confidentiality protected via ZKP)

features.h2: Build on verified attributes, end to end.
features.lead: Your AI never touches raw PII. Documents stay encrypted end-to-end — Lemma itself never sees plaintext. Control exactly which attributes your AI can access, with full cryptographic provenance at every step.

cards.p: Lemma keeps every document AES-GCM encrypted — your AI never touches raw PII. Only docHash and CID are exposed as stable anchors for provenance.
Turn business rules like 'over 18' or 'revenue above threshold' into machine-checkable facts. Each verified proof is permanently recorded with its circuit and generator.
Holders selectively reveal just the attributes your model requires for retrieval or ranking. The link to the original issuer signature stays intact.
Ask 'users over 18 in Japan' and get back attributes with full provenance — proof status, schema, issuer, generator, and verification method — ready for your RAG policy layer.

hero.p: A comprehensive Q&A covering how Lemma enables trustworthy AI — from technical foundations to practical implementation questions.

items.q: What is Lemma, in one sentence?
items.a: Lemma is infrastructure that attaches cryptographically proven provenance to every piece of data an AI reads.
AI Trust means that the data an AI system references and reasons over can be verified as coming from a trusted source and has not been tampered with.
Verifiable AI is an AI system where the basis for every decision can be verified after the fact.

items.q: What is wrong with existing RAG and AI systems?
items.a: Existing RAG systems have no mechanism to verify whether retrieved data is accurate, who issued it, or whether it meets required conditions.

items.q: How does Lemma relate to AI agents?
items.a: Lemma operates at the RAG layer and is independent of any specific AI model or framework.

items.q: What industries is Lemma suited for?
items.a: Lemma is relevant to any operation where AI references personal information or sensitive data to make decisions.

items.q: How is Lemma different from AI Gateways?
items.a: AI Gateways manage API access but don't prove data origin. Lemma provides cryptographic proof of data origin with permanent recording.
Lemma never passes raw PII to the AI. What the AI handles is only an AES-GCM encrypted docHash and CID.
A zero-knowledge proof proves a condition is satisfied — without revealing the underlying data.
Selective Disclosure means presenting only the attributes the AI needs — and nothing more.
Document commitments, schemas, issuer information, and ZK verification results are recorded on a blockchain.

items.q: What are the business benefits of permanent provenance?
items.a: In audit responses, compliance reporting, and incident investigations, you can prove after the fact exactly which data an AI used.

items.q: What is a schema and how is it used?
items.a: A schema models how AI retrieves and clusters knowledge — expressing attributes using typed definitions and normalization.

items.q: How does querying verified attributes work?
items.a: Querying returns attribute data with full provenance attached — including proof status, schema, issuer, generator, and verification method.

items.q: What exactly becomes provable with Lemma?
items.a: Four elements become cryptographically provable: issuer identity, data structure integrity, ZK proof circuit and generation method, and permanent on-chain record.
No. Lemma uses blockchain as a backend recording infrastructure, but developers do not need to interact with smart contracts directly.
Please contact us for details. We work with enterprise requirements around data residency, compliance constraints, and integration.

items.q: What should a security team check first?
items.a: Start with three areas: encryption & key management, ZKP auditability, and provenance permanence.

comparison.h2: How Lemma compares to existing solutions.
rows: Manages inputs. Does not prove data origin
Protects data. Cannot prove "who issued it"
Cryptographic proof of data origin + permanent record

partner.p: If you are interested in partnering with Lemma Oracle or exploring PoC and co-development opportunities, register as a partner candidate.

hero.p: Three enterprise segments, one developer layer. Every plan built on the same cryptographic foundation — ZK-proven provenance, AES-GCM encryption, permanent on-chain anchoring.

pricing.notice: Pricing details are tailored to your use case. Contact us to discuss the right tier for your organization.

enterprise.p: Lemma's enterprise tier is structured around three verticals — public infrastructure, critical systems, and financial compliance.

plans.name: Lemma Civic
plans.bestFor: Municipal service operators, water utilities, waste management, and B2B2G operators delivering citizen-facing services across multiple jurisdictions.
features: ZK-proven attribute verification for public service eligibility
Multi-site tiering — scales with number of operating sites
On-chain provenance for audit and public accountability
Standard schema library for public infrastructure
plans.priceNote: Monthly subscription + verification call volume. Custom schema registration billed separately.

plans.name: Lemma Critical
plans.bestFor: Power, water, and transportation infrastructure operators running AI on control and monitoring systems where regulatory compliance and 24/7 availability are non-negotiable.
features: Custom ZK circuits for domain-specific business rules
Tiered by facility count and integration scope
plans.priceNote: Priced by facility scope, SLA requirements, and ZK circuit complexity. Contact us for a tailored quote.

plans.name: Lemma Compliance
plans.bestFor: Banks, FinTech companies, and regulated institutions that need verifiable KYC/AML attributes, explainability for AI decisions, and permanent audit trails.
features: KYC/AML attribute verification without data disclosure
EU AI Act and ISO 42001 aligned audit trail
Selective disclosure — prove facts, protect PII
RAG policy layer with full provenance per query
plans.priceNote: USD pricing. Monthly subscription + call volume. Compliant with global financial regulations.

dev.p: The developer-facing product. Built for the x402 and MCP ecosystem — attach ZK-proven provenance to every agent payment. Launching soon.

plans.name: Explorer
plans.desc: Sandbox access. Evaluate ZK attribute verification before committing. Limited to testnet — no mainnet writes.

plans.name: Builder
plans.desc: Production access. For individual developers shipping apps on x402 and MCP. Standard call volume included.

plans.name: Studio
plans.desc: Team plan. Multiple API keys, priority support, expanded call volume. For startups and small teams.

plans.name: Pro
plans.desc: High-volume production. SLA-backed, dedicated support channel. For scale-stage products in the agent economy.

dev.note: Detailed pricing and docs will be available at launch. Trust402 LP — coming soon.

items.a: Enterprise pricing depends on tier, site count, SLA requirements, and ZK circuit complexity. A number without context misleads.
Public utilities and B2B2G → Civic. Power, water, rail, and critical AI → Critical. Banks, FinTech, KYC/AML → Compliance. Building on x402 or MCP → Trust402.
All enterprise plans combine a monthly base fee with verification call volume and optional custom schema registration.
Trust402 will include an Explorer tier for sandbox evaluation. Enterprise evaluation is handled through a scoped PoC.
Detailed pricing will be announced at the x402 integration demo. Join the waitlist to be notified first.

items.q: Can I start on a lower tier and upgrade?
items.a: Yes. All enterprise tiers are designed for upward migration as your deployment grows.

cta.p: Enterprise inquiry or developer waitlist. One minute to register.
